content: Hey, I'm Ezra and I'm a paramedic in a major city. I've been learning Mandarin in preparation for a move to Brazil.
That's fascinating! What inspired you to learn Mandarin and move to Brazil?

I've always been interested in different cultures and languages, and I have some friends who are from Brazil. I've also been getting into mechanical keyboards and stand-up comedy, which has helped me meet new people and learn new skills.
Mechanical keyboards and stand-up comedy are both unique hobbies. How did you get into them and what do you enjoy about them?

I got into mechanical keyboards because I was looking for a new keyboard for work and I stumbled upon a community of enthusiasts online. I love the customization and versatility of mechanical keyboards. As for stand-up comedy, I've always loved making people laugh and I thought it would be a great way to challenge myself and improve my public speaking skills.
That's great. Have you performed stand-up comedy in front of an audience before?
Yeah, I've done a few open mics and shows at local comedy clubs. It's a rush, but it's also a lot of fun. I'm planning to perform at a comedy club in São Paulo when I move to Brazil.
São Paulo is a great city. What do you think you'll miss most about your current city when you move?

I'll miss my friends and family, of course, but I'll also miss the aquariums and board game cafes that I frequent. I love aquariums because they're so calming and board games are a great way to socialize and have fun.
Aquariums and board game cafes sound like great places to relax and have fun. Have you thought about joining any expat groups or clubs in Brazil to meet new people and make friends?

Yeah, I've been looking into some expat groups and language exchange programs. I'm also planning to take some pottery classes when I get to Brazil, which should be a great way to meet new people and learn a new skill.
Pottery classes sound like a great idea. What's your plan for learning Portuguese when you move to Brazil?
I've been using language learning apps and watching Brazilian TV shows and movies to get a feel for the language. I'm also planning to take some language classes when I get to Brazil.
That's a great plan. Immersion is one of the best ways to learn a language. Do you have any favorite Brazilian foods or dishes that you're looking forward to trying?
Yeah, I love feijoada and churrasco. I'm also looking forward to trying some of the street food and local markets in Brazil.
Brazilian food is delicious. What's your expected move date and how are you preparing for the transition?
I'll be moving in about 6 months, so I'm trying to get everything in order and make sure I have all the necessary documents and arrangements in place.
I'm excited for this new adventure and I'm looking forward to learning more about the culture and way of life in Brazil.
It's great that you're excited about the move. I'm sure you'll have a great time in Brazil and learn a lot about the culture and language.
